name: ClimateTwinBench_DomainReasoning_v2
constraint: STRICT: Every answer derivable programmatically from dataset values alone. No external climate knowledge. No monsoon assumptions. No Paris thresholds. No pre-industrial baselines. No algorithm references. Ground truth sources: anomaly_score, rainfall_mm, risk_tier_schema (defined in code), national_yearly_stats.

questions.question:
Risk tier schema (defined by this dataset):
  LOW:|a|<2.00 | MODERATE:2.00≤|a|<5.00 | HIGH:5.00≤|a|<10.00 | EXTREME:|a|≥10.00

Cell (21.00°N, 74.75°E) — anomaly_score 2019–2025:
  2019:+3.392, 2020:-1.483, 2021:-0.006, 2022:-0.174, 2023:-0.815, 2024:-0.914, 2025:-0.631

In 2020 the cell's anomaly_score was -1.483.

Which risk tier applies?

options.A: EXTREME  — |anomaly_score| ≥ 10.00
options.B: LOW      — |anomaly_score| < 2.00
options.C: HIGH     — 5.00 ≤ |anomaly_score| < 10.00
options.D: MODERATE — 2.00 ≤ |anomaly_score| < 5.00

In 2022 (this cell's peak |anomaly| year):
  Cell anomaly_score    = +10.406
  National mean anomaly = +0.1030

Four compound signal labels are defined as follows:
  Systemic Dry      : both cell AND national mean negative
  Localised Wet/Dry : cell POSITIVE, national mean NEGATIVE
  Systemic Wet      : both cell AND national mean positive
  Localised Dry/Wet : cell NEGATIVE, national mean POSITIVE

Which label applies to this cell's 2022 event?

options.A: Systemic Wet       — cell anomaly POSITIVE AND national mean POSITIVE
options.B: Localised Wet/Dry  — cell anomaly POSITIVE,   national mean NEGATIVE
options.C: Systemic Dry       — cell anomaly NEGATIVE AND national mean NEGATIVE
options.D: Localised Dry/Wet  — cell anomaly NEGATIVE,   national mean POSITIVE

questions.explanation: Cell +10.406 (pos), National +0.1030 (pos). → SW: Systemic Wet       — cell anomaly POSITIVE AND national mean POSITIVE.

Risk tier schema: LOW:|a|<2.00 | MODERATE:2.00≤|a|<5.00 | HIGH:5.00≤|a|<10.00 | EXTREME:|a|≥10.00
An ESCALATION is a transition where risk tier rank strictly increases
(e.g. LOW→MODERATE, MODERATE→HIGH, HIGH→EXTREME, or LOW→HIGH).

Cell (31.25°N, 75.00°E) — anomaly_score 2019–2025:
  2019:+1.818, 2020:-0.830, 2021:+1.633, 2022:+0.372, 2023:-3.039, 2024:+0.046, 2025:+1.304

Applying the schema yields:
  2019:LOW, 2020:LOW, 2021:LOW, 2022:LOW, 2023:MODERATE, 2024:LOW, 2025:LOW

How many ESCALATIONS occurred across the 6 consecutive year-to-year transitions?
questions.explanation:
Transitions: 2019→2020:─, 2020→2021:─, 2021→2022:─, 2022→2023:↑ESC, 2023→2024:↓DSC, 2024→2025:─.
↑Escalations=1, ↓De-escalations=1, Total changes=2, Years at HIGH/EXTREME=0. Correct: 1.
